hero.bio: UX Designer & Strategic Communicator. Four languages, five organisations, one mission: making complexity human.

about.label: My story
about.title: From Languages to Design
paragraphs: My path started with languages. Studying Linguistic Mediation at the University of Turin gave me the tools to bridge cultures and communicate across boundaries. More than translating words, I learned to understand what people actually mean and what they truly need.

A Master's in International Relations and an Erasmus year in Lyon brought me face-to-face with the complexity of global cooperation. Working across organisations like Compassion Italia, Interreg ALCOTRA, CO.P.E. and Good Shepherd International Foundation confirmed something important: meaningful communication is never just about the message. It is about understanding people, their context and the barriers that keep them from what they need.
That insight drives my transition to UX Design. Listening deeply, simplifying complexity and crafting stories that connect across cultures: these are exactly the skills that great design demands.

projects.name: Digital Communication
projects.description: Amplifying the Foundation's voice across digital channels, managing social media and content to advance international advocacy and sustainable development goals.

projects.name: Development Cooperation Projects
projects.description: Steered development cooperation programmes across emerging countries by coordinating multi-stakeholder relations, overseeing project documentation and supporting grant reporting, ensuring operations stayed on track in complex international environments.

projects.name: Cross-border Communication
projects.description: Reported on the France-Italy ALCOTRA cross-border cooperation programme, producing institutional website updates and editing multilingual publications to keep communication clear and consistent across partner countries.

projects.name: Marketing & Communication
projects.description: Supported marketing and communication for a child development NGO, contributing to content creation, social media management and event promotion while maintaining a consistent and engaging presence across digital and in-person channels.

projects.name: Civil Service: Project Assistant
projects.description: Provided project coordination, youth tutoring and animation as part of a civil service internship, actively supporting the educational and community programmes of the oratory.

projects.name: Volunteer
projects.description: Volunteered as a tutor and youth support worker, offering one-to-one accompaniment and study support to young people participating in the oratory's community programmes.

elements.description: Specialised programme covering community management, content strategy and digital platform management.

elements.degree: Master in International Relations and Affairs
elements.description: Advanced programme in international relations, diplomacy and development cooperation with a focus on human and social sciences.
Erasmus exchange focused on international organisations, policy analysis and political science within a European academic context.
Undergraduate programme in linguistic mediation with a focus on French and intercultural communication.

contacts.label: Say hello
contacts.secondaryTitleText: Open to new opportunities and collaborations. Drop me a line. I always reply.
